action-paragraphs.structure: Five sentences building velocity: two short establishing urgency, two medium showing pursuit and arrival, one long climactic sentence landing the athletic feat.
rhetorical-dna: Percussive opening verb sets kinetic rhythm; sentences accelerate toward action
Sensory flashes (examples: cloak on wind, mount rearing) paint motion without slowing pace
Group reactions frame the figure - we see him through their startled eyes
Final sentence extends to match the action's arc, ending in final physical feat
action-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A moving figure makes a desperate maneuver onto a transport or through an obstacle.

action-paragraphs.structure: Four sentences of cascading collapse - each loss triggers the next, ending with terse takeover statement.
rhetorical-dna: Bodily detail under stress personalizes the doomed
Transport personified - loses headway, rolls helplessly when its handler falls
Rhythm mimics the domino-fall: steady, steady, then sudden stop with the sinking
Final sentence snaps to new subject, tersely announcing shift without elaboration
action-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A group falls one by one until a singular figure must act.

action-paragraphs.structure: Three sentences: brief setup, short pivot, then one long cascading sentence of carnage with accumulating verbs.
rhetorical-dna: Equipment given agency and sound (Example: sword sings its death-song)
Verbs pile in triadic and quadratic groups, each blow a percussive beat
Abstract states made physical (Example: fury as colored mist before the eyes)
Final clause lands on visceral tableau as summation
action-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure stands at the center of overwhelming violence and dominates through superior ferocity.

action-paragraphs.structure: Three sentences: first establishes dominance, second builds to expected destruction, third freezes all motion with external interruption.
rhetorical-dna: Tableau construction - action halts into frozen image
Parallel final clause balances opposing forces: foes poised, actor ready
Momentum built specifically to be arrested; the interruption gains power from denied climax
Single sensory detail sufficient to halt the scene
action-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A standoff reaches its breaking point and is frozen by unexpected intervention.

scene-description-paragraphs.structure: Six sentences building from conditional environment observation to accumulated images of decay. Opens with elaborate subordinate clause, middle sentences layer parasitic imagery, closes with terse day/night contrast.
rhetorical-dna: Elaborate periodic sentences give way to shorter declarative punch at the end
Disease and parasitism metaphors (epidemic, swarmed, clamping like limpets)
Anthropomorphic violence applied to environment (examples: mutilated finger, fists of knuckled masonry, pointed blasphemously)
Accumulation through shadows: shadows of smaller features, then a dominant penumbra cast from  a large structure or geological feature
Final sentence splits into binary contrast (night/day, voice/voiceless)
scene-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A grand environment (natural or built) overwhelmed by dependent growths, culminating in one dominant feature that commands the scene.

scene-description-paragraphs.structure: Five sentences descending from elaborate observation to sentence-fragment finality. Opens with complex participial construction, compresses through middle, ends with three-word verdict.
rhetorical-dna: Paradox as central device (vivid yet dark, colorful yet exuding darkness)
Atmosphere given emotional weight (air turgid with contempt)
Simile deflates human/object figures (examples: craftsmen like beggars)
Progressive shortening mirrors degradation: long sentence, medium, short, shorter, fragment
Final fragment delivers blunt judgment after ornate buildup
scene-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A gathering of people and/or objects that, despite surface vitality, emanates decay and emotional poison.

scene-description-paragraphs.structure: Two sentences of measured, slow observation. Both begin with light's attempt and acknowledge its failure, yet the tone softens rather than despairs.
rhetorical-dna: Light as agent that partially fails (examples: gave vague structure, in no way dispelled)
Extended simile elevates dust motes, rays, or glimmers to cosmic imagery
Warm and brooding coexist without contradiction
Latinate diction creates formal, almost liturgical register (examples: infiltration, attenuate, grave order)
Rhythm slows through polysyllables, creating contemplative suspension
scene-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: Light penetrates a dark space, revealing small details that suggest larger, slower order.

scene-description-paragraphs.structure: Three sentences establishing spatial and comparative relationship between connected locations. Opens with superlative claim, follows with elaborate conditional syntax explaining the comparison, closes by contrasting the third space with its superior illumination and sweeping vista.
rhetorical-dna: Comparative framework structures observation (example: darkest, less direct, best lit)
Light treated as active agent that penetrates, infiltrates through decay (examples: fissures, dislodged portions)
Final sentence rewards patience with panoramic payoff after cramped observations
Scale collapse for dramatic effect (example: figure no taller than a thimble)
Latinate and technical diction (examples: penetrate, banistered verandah, bastions, quadrangle)
scene-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A sequence of connected spaces, each characterized by how light reaches it, culminating in one that opens onto a vast view.

scene-description-paragraphs.structure: Seven sentences building from simple action through expanding revelation of space and its accumulated contents. Opens with brief physical gesture, pivots to unconscious affection, then unfolds into elaborate cataloguing of objects that threatens to overwhelm before revealing secret pathways through.
rhetorical-dna: Small domestic gesture grounds vast description in bodily scale
Catalogue technique: accumulation through diverse objects from grand to trivial
Paradox of constriction within vastness (example: long and lofty yet appearing smaller)
Extended simile comparing intimate knowledge to indigenous expertise
Sentence length mirrors spatial journey: short entrance, expanding middle, long winding exploration
scene-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure enters a cluttered/dense space whose apparent chaos conceals secret passages known only to them.

scene-description-paragraphs.structure: Three sentences moving from singular striking object to character's response to that object. Opens with central artifact described in isolation, middle sentence captures impulsive physical action, final sentence traces restless eyes seeking focus.
rhetorical-dna: Polished grotesque as focal point (examples: writhing root, rare gloss, every wrinkle gleaming)
Objects/features given sensory personality (example: suavity of contour)
Eyes given independence and agency, moving as if separate from their owner
Tonal shift: calm emotion upon entry now replaced by smouldering restlessness
Neither setting nor object can hold attention, suggesting internal turbulence
scene-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure sprawls in an ornate or sacral space, their restless gaze finding no anchor among the striking objects/features around them.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Seven sentences building from garment outward to physique to bearing to face, concluding with eye-contact confrontation. Short declarative opener, then increasingly elaborate dependent clauses stacking physical detail, climaxing in a power dynamic.
rhetorical-dna: Anatomical inventory moving outward then inward: clothing, body, features, finally eyes
Simile anchors physicality (example: 'broad as a bull's'), sensory luxury (example: 'made for wine-cups and for ladies' lips')
Deliberate archaisms and compound epithets signal high register
Tension created through contrast (example: subject's insolence 'tamed' by superior presence)
Triadic listing at phrase level (example: 'throat and neck,' 'chin and jaw') gives cadence weight
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A proud warrior or professional presents himself before an imposing/authoritative figure.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Two sentences only. First establishes scale through spatial filling; second delivers character impression via participial phrases stacked after the main clause.
rhetorical-dna: Economy of means: entire presence conveyed in two strokes
Physical mass implied through architecture (example: 'filled all the space between the pillars')
Present participles chain consequence (example: 'clothed... armed... smoking')—suggests action just completed
Register formal but punchy; no ornamentation, raw impression
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A formidable figure occupies a seat of power, fresh from violence.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Long accumulative paragraph opening with movement, then static inventory of attire, then extended simile, finally settling on face and expression. Sentences lengthen progressively; last sentence is the longest, weaving contradictions.
rhetorical-dna: Animal simile opens (example: 'lithe beast of prey newly wakened'), establishing predatory grace
Feminine comparison recurs (examples: 'slender... like a girl,' 'softly coloured like a girl's face') creating deliberate contrast with martial hints
Attire catalogued with precise materials and colors (examples: silk, gold, specific gemstones)
Nature simile (example: 'delicate peak of a snow mountain') bridges delicacy and latent power
Final sentence braids contradictions (examples: melancholy/disdain, gentleness/fiery glints, beauty/determination)
Archaic inversions (example: 'Very tall was that lord') maintain elevated register throughout
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure of deceptive delicacy appears, concealing dangerous capability beneath graceful beauty.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Two sentences. First traces movement through alternating light; second expands into personification, the environment responding to the figure. Climactic clause stacks contrasts (example: outward loveliness vs. inner fire).
rhetorical-dna: Nature personified as celebrant (example: 'spring laughed for joy beholding')
Contrast between external grace and internal vitality (examples: 'loveliness and grace' vs. 'fire and vital sap')
Elemental vocabulary (sunlight, shine, shade, spring, sap) creates organic unity
Movement implied through spatial progression (example: 'shine to shade and to shine again')
Register elevated but warm; admiring rather than awed
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A vital figure moves through an environment that seems to welcome or celebrate their presence.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Four sentences building portrait through contradiction. Opens with manner (example: furtive yet goodly), then anatomical features with animal similes, closes on spare frame and coloring. Each sentence adds one or two precise details.
rhetorical-dna: Opening paradox sets tension (example: 'furtive step yet goodly and fair')
Animal comparisons recur: sickle (predatory tool), ox eyes (inscrutable bulk), retriever coat (groomed darkness)
Physical descriptors lean toward the uncanny: (example: 'hook, lean, spare, pale' repeated)
Triadic structure at phrase level (examples: 'lean and spare,' 'great and fair')
Register formal, clinical; observer maintaining distance
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure approaches whose pleasing appearance contradicts something furtive or predatory in their manner.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Three sentences of increasing menace. First places figure in setting with posture; second lingers on atmospheric lighting; third delivers extended anatomical inventory culminating in eyes. Main clause delayed by participial buildup.
rhetorical-dna: Predatory animal imagery throughout (examples: 'lean as an rat's tail,' 'hooked as the eagle's beak')
Light personified as co-conspirator (example: 'mother of shade and secrecy... moved about')
Anatomical inventory builds threat
Final image of eyes completes menacing portrait
Archaic syntax and compound epithets maintain high register
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A powerful figure sits in shadow, their predatory features gradually revealed by unfriendly light.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Four sentences cataloguing attire. Each sentence names materials, colors, and ornamentation with lapidary precision. No verbs of action; static display.
rhetorical-dna: Catalog structure: different parts of attire, in sequence
Precious materials accumulated (examples: chain mail, gold, hyacinths, opals, diamonds, sealskin, ruby, cobra skins, silk)
Symbolic detail embedded
Color palette restricted to two primary colors throughout, with single accent
Register heraldic; reading like blazon or inventory rather than narrative
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure's elaborate outfit is catalogued piece by piece, revealing wealth, power, and symbolic identity.

character-description-paragraphs.structure: Five sentences alternating between physical impression and attire. Final image synthesizes light/dark contrast.
rhetorical-dna: Martial simile for feminine subject (example: 'brave of carriage like a war-horse')
Contradictions woven throughout (examples: 'soft of feature' but 'proud-eyed,' 'sweet-mouthed, but not as one who can speak nought but sweetness')
Attire described with textile precision (examples: buff silk, spider-web gold threads, point-lace, wire stiffening)
Natural imagery for hair (examples: raven's wing, yellow rose, moon among night clouds)
Register courtly, admiring but wary; subject commands respect rather than inviting intimacy
character-description-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A beautiful woman is described, her softness undercut by manly qualities (examples: brave, proud).

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Five long sentences of address, each building on the last. Opens with reassurance, traces through recent calamity to present opportunity, climaxes with tactical proposal.
rhetorical-dna: Archaizing syntax with inverted word order (IE: 'rejoiced I,' 'jumpeth with mine own designs')
Periodic sentence structure that withholds the main point until the end
Courtly register: formal address, elaborate honorifics
Climactic accumulation toward a proposed action
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: An actor addresses their master/leader, turning disaster into opportunity through cunning proposal.

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Extended catalogue of terrors, each episode punctuated by the same refrain, building through escalating wonders to a final vision of sublime beauty.
rhetorical-dna: Anaphoric refrain creates drumbeat rhythm across escalating dangers
Each episode more cosmic than the last: beasts, cataclysm, supernatural invasion, haunted wilderness
Tonal pivot at the end from terror-catalogue to transcendent landscape vision
Sensory density: starlight, volcanic fire, ghostly voices, ice-robed peaks
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A catalogue of terrors survived by an actor to establish their fitness for a dangerous task.

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Three movements: acknowledges enemy strength through past defeats, pivots to actor's unique advantage, ends with ominous caveat. Balanced clauses weigh loss against hope.
rhetorical-dna: Grudging admission of enemy prowess maintains tension through honesty
Historical examples establish pattern of defeat despite physical supremacy
Rhetorical pivot shifts from despair to menace
Final sentence undercuts confidence with danger, creating unease
Formal, ceremonial diction befitting a leader's inner counsel
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A leader acknowledges past defeats to introduce a dangerous new strategy.

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Opens with portentous identification of artifact, recounts predecessor's doom in elaborate detail, then pivots to present intention and request. Ends with a challenge.
rhetorical-dna: Nested doom-narrative creates weight of history
Accumulative 'either...or' structure multiplies possible failures
Archaic vocabulary heightens ritual atmosphere
Confidence despite acknowledged peril creates dramatic irony
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: An explanation of the dangers of an actor's art.

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Single flowing sentence of journey, linking departure to arrival through named waypoints. Compressed time, expanded geography.
rhetorical-dna: Paratactic 'and so...and so' structure mimics the steady pace of travel
Place-names create texture without slowing narrative
Efficient time-skip signals transition
Unpunctuated flow suggests uneventful passage
Grounding function: moves characters between dramatic scenes
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: Travelers journey between locations while sharing news of prior events.

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Opens with night's safety, blooms into elaborate dawn imagery, then shifts to grueling traverse. Accumulative hardships build through the day to exhausted arrival.
rhetorical-dna: Lyrical nature imagery gives way to harsh terrain catalog
Comparative intensification builds severity
Personification of landscape and elements
Sensory accumulation: color, temperature, texture, time
Physical toll registered in specific bodily details
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A party endures a dangerous mountain crossing from shelter to destination.

character-exposition-paragraphs.structure: Two sentences: first describes provisions through sensory detail and effect, second elevates to divine register. Compression after prior paragraph's exhaustion.
rhetorical-dna: Sensory triptych (appearance, texture, taste) grounds the magical in physical experience
Comparison to ordinary food heightens supernatural restoration
Color imagery links sustenance to cosmic beauty
Brevity after ordeal creates pacing relief
Closing invocation of divine peace signals resolution
character-exposition-paragraphs.respecification-seed: Exhausted travelers consume restorative provisions after ordeal.

gro-reflection-paragraphs.structure: Opens with rhetorical question anticipating judgment, pivots to self-defense through enumeration of hard-won achievements. One long unwinding sentence establishing stakes.
rhetorical-dna: Inverted syntax and archaic constructions create formal, deliberate cadence
Self-aware framing: speaker pre-empts criticism by voicing it first
Triadic listing builds cumulative weight
Intimate metaphor for bond with authority figure
gro-reflection-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure defends a dangerous choice made at the height of hard-won security.

gro-reflection-paragraphs.structure: Opens with defiant reversal, expands into philosophical maxim, then unfolds into extended allegory. Rhythm shifts from terse declaration to lyrical accumulation.
rhetorical-dna: Proverbial wisdom as foundation
Cosmic allegory (example: day vs. night as triumph over darkness)
Personification building in intensity: delicate to wild to triumphant
Parallel construction cascading into celebration
Register shifts from sardonic to exalted
gro-reflection-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A character dismisses conventional wisdom by invoking nature's own cycles of triumph and loss.

gro-reflection-paragraphs.structure: Two-part movement: first, rhetorical question challenging loyalty to success; second, philosophical self-justification through metaphor.
rhetorical-dna: Defiant self-defense through counter-question
Extended metaphor linking personal loyalty to cosmic phenomena
Archaic formality lending dignity to what might seem betrayal
Final clause lifts into abstraction
gro-reflection-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A figure justifies their questionable choices by an extended metaphor with divine/natural phenomenon.

gro-reflection-paragraphs.structure: Opens with aphoristic statement, then develops through direct address and extended climbing metaphor. Builds through hypotheticals to grim conclusion.
rhetorical-dna: Maxim as thesis
Direct address creating intimate tutorial tone
Life-as-X metaphor sustained through multiple scenarios
Rhetorical questions driving argument forward
Dark humor in final image (Example: corpse vs. successful climber)
gro-reflection-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A character proposes that indirect paths succeed where direct ones lead to ruin or death.

gro-reflection-paragraphs.structure: Formal address establishing debt, pivoting to offer of counsel. Single building sentence leading to promise of singular solution.
rhetorical-dna: Courtly formula opening
Parallel construction in middle
Tease structure: many options considered, one chosen
Formal register throughout, deferential but confident
gro-reflection-paragraphs.respecification-seed: An character makes a pledge and promises a single best choice after much deliberation.

gro-reflection-paragraphs.structure: Opens with chiding observation, proceeds through dismissal of obvious strengths, builds to revelation of hidden greater power. Three beats: critique, enumeration, climactic mystery.
rhetorical-dna: Proverbial wisdom as framing device
Deliberate undercutting of military assets to elevate something intangible
Comparative structure building to superlative
Suspense through withholding: the greater thing unnamed
Direct address maintaining intimate advisory tone
gro-reflection-paragraphs.respecification-seed: A character warns against relying on obvious strength, hinting at a hidden greater asset.
